title: Why we have electoral college and should abolish it

enrichment.summary: Kyle explains the historical origins of the Electoral College as a deliberate aristocratic check on pure democracy, tracing the idea through Aristotle, Rome, Montesquieu, and the Federalist Papers. He argues the Electoral College has been co-opted by oligarchy rather than educated elites as intended, and that combined with violations of the emoluments and rebellion clauses, the constitutional framework is unraveling. He concludes the Electoral College should be abolished because modern information access makes its original justification obsolete.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Should be abolished — its original purpose is obsolete and it now serves oligarchic interests
Has been effectively ignored, allowing oligarchs to buy political power
Violated by Trump running again after January 6th, setting a dangerous precedent
A mix of democracy, aristocracy, and kingship is historically the most stable system
Because everyone can now be equally informed, the aristocratic justification for the Electoral College no longer applies
Breaking one clause sets a precedent that unravels the whole document
